species: three-species community (Clostridium cellulolyticum + Desulfovibrio vulgaris + Geobacter sulfurreducens)

growth_records.strain: C. cellulolyticum H10 (ATCC 35319); D. vulgaris Hildenborough (NCIMB 8303); G. sulfurreducens

up.method: D x consumed = 0.03/h x ~4.9 mM (complete removal)

sec.method: D x ~3.6 mM (from 1.63 mol/mol cellobiose x 2.2 mM)
detected in N2 headspace; 3.3 mol CO2 per mol cellobiose (model)

mfa.units: mM steady state (2.7 mol per mol cellobiose)
mfa.note: major fermentation product; ~0.7 mol/mol went to Geobacter/biomass
combined C. cellulolyticum + G. sulfurreducens (D. vulgaris has incomplete TCA)

mfa.units: mM consumed (2.7 mol per mol cellobiose)
mfa.note: barium-chloride assay; 2 mM residual -> electron-donor limited; reduced to H2S
from fumarate; 0.02 mol malate also detected

growth_records.cite: Miller, Lance D et al. Establishment and metabolic analysis of a model microbial community for understanding trophic and electron accepting interactions of subsurface anaerobic environments. BMC Microbiology (2010). DOI:10.1186/1471-2180-10-149. [PMC2906461]
growth_records.snippet: the 2.2 mM cellobiose decreased to less than 0.5 mM within 2 days... Cellobiose fermentation resulted primarily in the production of acetate and CO2... D. vulgaris depleted 6.1 mM sulfate (out of the 8 mM supplied)... 2.23 moles of fumarate
growth_records.notes: Anaerobic continuous co-culture chemostat, D=0.03/h (flow 0.34 mL/min, ~650 mL), 30C, pH 7.0-7.2 (uncontrolled). Steady-state OD600=0.4, biomass 236 mg/L=0.236 gDW/L. mu=D=0.03/h at steady state; doubling=ln2/0.03=23.1 h. Volumetric exchange rates computed as D x (feed-residual): cellobiose 0.03x2.2=0.066 mmol/L/h; sulfate 0.03x6.1=0.183; fumarate 0.03x4.9=0.147; acetate 0.03x5.93=0.178. Per-community-gDW using 0.236 gDW/L. Per-organism model values (Fig 5) are per mole cellobiose. H2 <0.3 uM (below detection); ethanol trace. Biomass formula C4H7O1.5N (104 g/mol).
